verses.text: Paul has 'great heaviness' for his kinsmen, wishing himself accursed for them. Deep love. He grieves for Israel's unbelief with a Christlike heart.
Paul has 'great heaviness' for his kinsmen, wishing himself accursed for them. Deep love. He grieves for Israel's unbelief with a Christlike heart.
Paul has 'great heaviness' for his kinsmen, wishing himself accursed for them. Deep love. He grieves for Israel's unbelief with a Christlike heart.
To them belong the adoption, covenants, and promises; of them Christ came, 'who is over all, God blessed for ever.' Rich heritage. Christ Himself is owned as God.
To them belong the adoption, covenants, and promises; of them Christ came, 'who is over all, God blessed for ever.' Rich heritage. Christ Himself is owned as God.
Not all Israel are Israel; 'in Isaac shall thy seed be called'; the children of promise are the seed. True children. Descent does not make one God's child.
Not all Israel are Israel; 'in Isaac shall thy seed be called'; the children of promise are the seed. True children. Descent does not make one God's child.
Not all Israel are Israel; 'in Isaac shall thy seed be called'; the children of promise are the seed. True children. Descent does not make one God's child.
Not all Israel are Israel; 'in Isaac shall thy seed be called'; the children of promise are the seed. True children. Descent does not make one God's child.
Before the twins did good or evil, 'Jacob have I loved, but Esau have I hated,' that election might stand. Sovereign choice. God's purpose rests on His will, not man's works.
Before the twins did good or evil, 'Jacob have I loved, but Esau have I hated,' that election might stand. Sovereign choice. God's purpose rests on His will, not man's works.
Before the twins did good or evil, 'Jacob have I loved, but Esau have I hated,' that election might stand. Sovereign choice. God's purpose rests on His will, not man's works.
Before the twins did good or evil, 'Jacob have I loved, but Esau have I hated,' that election might stand. Sovereign choice. God's purpose rests on His will, not man's works.
Is God unrighteous? God forbid; 'it is not of him that willeth... but of God that sheweth mercy.' Mercy free. Salvation springs from God's mercy alone.
Is God unrighteous? God forbid; 'it is not of him that willeth... but of God that sheweth mercy.' Mercy free. Salvation springs from God's mercy alone.
Is God unrighteous? God forbid; 'it is not of him that willeth... but of God that sheweth mercy.' Mercy free. Salvation springs from God's mercy alone.
He raised up Pharaoh to show His power; He 'hath mercy on whom he will,' and hardens whom He will. God's right. He is free to show mercy or to harden.
He raised up Pharaoh to show His power; He 'hath mercy on whom he will,' and hardens whom He will. God's right. He is free to show mercy or to harden.
Who art thou that 'repliest against God'? has not the potter power over the clay? Be still. The creature may not arraign the Creator.
Who art thou that 'repliest against God'? has not the potter power over the clay? Be still. The creature may not arraign the Creator.
Who art thou that 'repliest against God'? has not the potter power over the clay? Be still. The creature may not arraign the Creator.
He endured the vessels of wrath, to show His riches on 'the vessels of mercy,' Jews and Gentiles. Patient purpose. His long-suffering serves His grace.
He endured the vessels of wrath, to show His riches on 'the vessels of mercy,' Jews and Gentiles. Patient purpose. His long-suffering serves His grace.
He endured the vessels of wrath, to show His riches on 'the vessels of mercy,' Jews and Gentiles. Patient purpose. His long-suffering serves His grace.
As in Hosea, 'I will call them my people, which were not my people.' Grace to outsiders. The far-off are made His own.
As in Hosea, 'I will call them my people, which were not my people.' Grace to outsiders. The far-off are made His own.
Isaiah cried, though Israel be as the sand, 'a remnant shall be saved.' A saved remnant. God preserves His own through judgment.
Isaiah cried, though Israel be as the sand, 'a remnant shall be saved.' A saved remnant. God preserves His own through judgment.
Isaiah cried, though Israel be as the sand, 'a remnant shall be saved.' A saved remnant. God preserves His own through judgment.
The Gentiles attained righteousness by faith; Israel, seeking by works, 'stumbled at that stumblingstone.' Stumbled. They missed Christ by trusting their own law-keeping.
The Gentiles attained righteousness by faith; Israel, seeking by works, 'stumbled at that stumblingstone.' Stumbled. They missed Christ by trusting their own law-keeping.
